name: COSMOS multivitamin modestly slowed selected epigenetic clocks while cocoa was null
summary: COSMOS provides recent large randomized evidence for small multivitamin effects on selected second-generation clocks, with no cocoa-extract effect.
statement: In the COSMOS epigenetic-clock ancillary study, daily multivitamin-multimineral supplementation modestly slowed PCGrimAge and PCPhenoAge, while cocoa extract did not affect the tested clocks.
population_or_model: Older adults in the COSMOS epigenetic-clock ancillary study
quantitative_note: The abstract reports yearly between-group differences of -0.113 years for PCGrimAge and -0.214 years for PCPhenoAge, with no cocoa effect across five clocks.
caveats: The clock effects were statistically significant but small.
The trial does not establish whether clock changes explain chronic-disease outcomes.
Only selected clocks moved, and cocoa extract was null.
The study had supplement-industry in-kind support and related investigator disclosures.
